title: Playground Run Monitor
concept: A diagnostics-first Playground redesign for educators running workshops: the first viewport is a run monitor with environment health, live logs, database state, and a compact live WordPress preview, while creation, saving, saved management, imports, exports, and gallery browsing live in a top-navigation contextual drawer.
focus: light gray workspace with green status accents
database and log visibility in the first viewport
flows: Monitor Playground health, boot progress, logs, database state, and live WordPress navigation from the first screen.

Start Playgrounds from vanilla WordPress, WordPress PR, Gutenberg PR or branch, GitHub import, Blueprint URL, .zip import, featured blueprints, or the full Blueprint gallery through the contextual drawer.
Save a temporary Playground to browser storage or a local directory with visible file-copy progress.
Manage saved and unsaved Playgrounds with rename, delete, and save actions.
Use Site Manager sections for runtime settings, file browsing and code editing, Blueprint editing, database tools, and logs.
Export to GitHub, download a .zip, import a .zip, copy or download Blueprint bundles, run Blueprints, and download database.sqlite.
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
refresh the active embedded WordPress page
edit WordPress version, PHP version, and language settings
understand destructive reset and stored Save & Reload behavior
use File browser with create file, create folder, upload, browse, and code editing affordances
view, copy, download, and run the current Blueprint bundle
inspect database driver, path, and size
inspect Playground, WordPress, and PHP logs with empty states
